remoteInstancesPage.title: Remote Instances
remoteInstancesPage.selectInstance: Select an instance from the sidebar or import one from SSH config.
remoteInstancesPage.actionsDescription: Connect, inspect logs, and manage this instance.
remoteInstancesPage.remoteServerDescription: How OpenChamber is discovered or started on the remote machine.
remoteInstancesPage.managedHint: Managed mode auto-starts OpenChamber on the remote machine.
remoteInstancesPage.portHint: Port used when launching OpenChamber remotely in managed mode.
remoteInstancesPage.installMethodHint: How OpenChamber gets installed or updated remotely when mode is Managed.
remoteInstancesPage.keepServerRunningHint: If enabled, OpenChamber daemon is left running remotely when you disconnect.
remoteInstancesPage.mainTunnelDescription: Primary local URL that points to the remote OpenChamber server.
remoteInstancesPage.bindHostHint: Network interface for the main local URL. Use 127.0.0.1 or localhost for local-only access.
remoteInstancesPage.preferredLocalPortHint: Preferred local port for the main OpenChamber tunnel. Leave empty for auto-select.
remoteInstancesPage.authenticationDescription: Optional credentials for SSH and remote UI.
remoteInstancesPage.portForwardsDescription: Optional extra SSH forwards in addition to the primary OpenChamber tunnel.
remoteInstancesPage.localListenHint: Local host and port where this forward listens on your machine.
remoteInstancesPage.localTargetHint: Local host and port on your machine that receives traffic from remote -R listener.
remoteInstancesPage.remoteListenHint: Remote host and port where SSH creates the -R listener.
remoteInstancesPage.remoteTargetHint: Remote host and port that receives traffic from local -L listener.
remoteInstancesPage.retryRandomPort: Retried with a random local port
remoteInstancesPage.urlNotAvailable: Instance URL is not available yet
remoteInstancesPage.binding0Warning: Binding local forwards to 0.0.0.0 makes the forwarded port reachable from other devices on your network. Continue?
remoteInstancesPage.binding0WarningMain: Binding to 0.0.0.0 exposes forwarded ports to your local network. Continue?
remoteInstancesPage.storePasswordPlaintext: Store SSH password in settings.json as plaintext?
remoteInstancesPage.storeOpenchamberPassword: Store OpenChamber UI password in settings.json as plaintext?
remoteInstancesPage.portInUse: Local port is already in use. Pick a random free local port and retry?
remoteInstancesPage.forwardTypeRemoteDesc: Remote (-R): expose a port on the remote machine and send that traffic back to this laptop.
remoteInstancesPage.forwardTypeDynamicDesc: Dynamic (-D): create a local SOCKS5 proxy on this laptop (for apps that support SOCKS proxy settings).
remoteInstancesPage.forwardTypeLocalDesc: Local (-L): open a port on this laptop and send it to a remote host:port over SSH (use this to access remote services locally).
remoteInstancesPage.forwardTypeHint: Local (-L): laptop -> remote service. Remote (-R): remote machine -> this laptop. Dynamic (-D): local SOCKS5 proxy.

tunnelSettings.title: Remote Tunnel
tunnelSettings.description: Configure secure remote access with quick links or your own named Cloudflare tunnel.

tunnelSettings.reliableAccess: For more reliable long-lived access, switch to Named Tunnel mode.

tunnelSettings.namedTunnelRequires: Named tunnels require a bought domain in your Cloudflare account.

tunnelSettings.startTunnelInfo: Start a {{mode}} tunnel and generate a one-time connect link. Do not close the app while this tunnel is in use.
tunnelSettings.installInstructions: Install it to enable remote tunnel access:
tunnelSettings.quickTooltip: Quick Tunnel is best effort and Cloudflare does not guarantee uptime. For more reliable long-lived access, switch to Named Tunnel mode.
tunnelSettings.namedTooltip: Named Tunnel mode uses your Cloudflare account and custom hostname for more reliable remote access.
tunnelSettings.failedSaveTtl: Failed to save tunnel TTL settings
tunnelSettings.failedSaveToken: Failed to save named tunnel token
